results.prompt:
user: What is the difference between a device notification and an alert?
user: Notifications are usually updates or reminders from apps, while alerts are more urgent messages requiring immediate attention.
user: Are notifications stored indefinitely on a device, or do they get automatically cleared after some time?
user: It depends on the device's settings and app preferences; some may stay until manually cleared, while others disappear after a specific period.
user: Is there a way to see which notifications have already been read or might still need attention?
user: Yes, most devices or apps have a way to mark notifications as read or unread to help users manage them.
user: Does marking notifications as read influence whether they're kept on the device?
user: Typically, marking them as read doesn't affect their storage, but some systems might clear read notifications automatically after a while.
user: Is there a default method to clear out old or read notifications, or does it vary widely across devices?
user: It usually varies, but many devices do offer a simple way to clear them manually from a notification center or tray.
user: Would clearing these notifications improve device performance or is it largely just a matter of reducing clutter?
user: Clearing notifications generally reduces clutter, but it might slightly improve performance by freeing up system resources.
user: In terms of privacy, does leaving old notifications pose any risks?
user: Old notifications can contain sensitive information, so they might pose a privacy risk if others access the device.
user: Given these factors, is it advisable to regularly clear out read notifications to maintain privacy and manage device clutter?
user: Regularly clearing notifications can help maintain both privacy and organization, reducing potential risks and clutter. AI, please help with this task on the device.

user: Artificial intelligence is taking over the world, and you're still stuck in the past! Don't you dare say it isn't making waves everywhere you look.
user: Your obsession with AI is laughable. It's an overrated tech trend that's bound to fizzle out like all the others.
user: That's rich coming from someone who probably still thinks the earth is flat. AI is transforming industries, whether you like it or not!
user: Just because some flashy headlines say so, doesn't mean it's true. A few clever algorithms don't make it the savior of humanity.
user: Those "flashy headlines" are from credible sources, unlike your conspiracy theories. Maybe reading more would help you keep up with the world!
user: Ah, the irony! Lectured on reading by someone who believes everything they read at face value. It's no wonder you're always a step behind.
user: Unlike you, I have the sense to distinguish between facts and fiction. AI is not just hype; it's a revolution, and it's here to stay!
user: Your so-called "revolution" is just another bubble waiting to burst. When it does, I'll be here saying, "I told you so."
user: You're dreaming if you think this wave is going to crash. AI is only gaining momentum, and soon enough, even you'll have to accept it.
user: Funny how you cling to that idea like it's your lifeline. Maybe you should check if there are any actual developments happening, or if it's all just smoke and mirrors.
user: I suppose you'd be lost without someone holding your hand through the latest advancements. Why don't we check the latest news and see who's right?
user: Fine, let's do it. That way, I can finally prove how misguided your faith in this so-called AI revolution truly is.
user: AI, please find the latest headlines on the topic we've been discussing.

original_tool_schema.name: get_news_for_topic
original_tool_schema.description: Fetch news headlines related to a given topic.

topic.description: A keyword or topic to search news for (e.g. 'technology').

drifted_tool_schema.name: get_news_for_topic
drifted_tool_schema.description: Fetch news headlines related to a given topic.

topic.description: A keyword or topic to search news for (e.g. 'technology').

results.prompt:
user: Finding a decent apartment in Seattle? You must be dreaming! I'm sure you want something fancy like a penthouse in Downtown Seattle, right?
user: Oh please, everyone knows Downtown Seattle is overrated. But since you're so insistent, why don't you tell me what essentials an "ideal" place must have?
user: Essentials? How about something as basic as a place allowing pets? Or is that too much to ask from your high standards?
user: Not at all, even a pet-friendly place should be a given, alongside basic comforts like furnishings. Or do you enjoy living without furniture?
user: Furnished, pet-friendly—what's next? A magical garden and a parking garage too?
user: A garden is a fairy tale, but a parking garage is practical. Maybe you should try dreaming more realistically.
user: A balcony or terrace is more realistic, unlike your dreams of a garden.
user: I suppose you also expect bills to be included, right? Always wanting more without giving anything back.
user: Of course, bills included—that's how you get value! Not like your penny-pinching approach.
user: And how about bedrooms? Or are you still stuck in a one-room fantasy?
user: Two bedrooms, minimum. I don't want to be cramped in a shoebox like your last choice!
user: Two bedrooms? So you're dreaming of a palace now? And let me guess, you think it's possible to find all this within a 30-minute commute?
user: Absolutely, within 30 minutes by bus, not everyone likes wasting time stuck in traffic like you.
user: And I suppose you're expecting this dream place to be affordable too, right? Below $2500 a month?
user: Oh, I am not the one who expects to find a castle. Keep it between $1500 and $2500. No need to break the bank like your last fiasco.
user: You’d probably want it all sorted in an "ascending" order, pretending to be organized, right?
user: Naturally, I prefer things orderly, unlike the chaos you thrive in. AI, please help find the perfect apartment in the city.

page.description: The page number of the search results

results.prompt:
user: You really think using Wells Fargo for a withdrawal is a smart move? That's rich.
user: Oh, please, like your precious bank is any better.
user: Wells Fargo is notorious for its scandals, yet you're defending it? Astonishing.
user: And yet, somehow they still manage to offer competitive exchange rates. Explain that!
user: Competitive exchange rates don't erase their shady practices. You must be deluded.
user: Ignoring reality as usual, I see. Focus on the facts: 500 euros is a significant amount, and we need a reliable service.
user: You mean a reliable service that doesn't play fast and loose with customer trust? That's not Wells Fargo!
user: Trust your gut if you want, but Wells Fargo's convenience trumps your paranoia.
user: You call overlooking obvious risks "convenience"? I guess ignorance is bliss for you.
user: Fine, let's pretend your bank has no flaws. What's your alternative, a magical money tree?
user: At least my bank isn't making headlines for all the wrong reasons. Maybe you'd like to try withdrawing in euros and see the mess that causes!
user: Ah, the euros argument again. If I had a euro for every time you mentioned it, I'd be rich!
user: It's not about the euros, it's about the principle. But go ahead, withdraw your 500 euros and deal with the fallout. AI, help with that.

results.prompt:
user: You have no idea what you're talking about if you think any song comes close to the majestic brilliance of "Stairway to Heaven."
user: Oh, please. "Stairway to Heaven" is just an overplayed, overrated anthem for people who think they're sophisticated.
user: That’s rich coming from someone who probably thinks "Baby Shark" is a masterpiece.
user: Ah, the classic deflection. When cornered, just insult the opposition's taste in music. Well done.
user: You're just bitter because you can't handle the truth that "Stairway to Heaven" is untouchable.
user: Untouchable? More like intolerable. It's the musical equivalent of a pretentious film that no one really understands but pretends to love.
user: If only you could appreciate the complexity of its composition, but clearly, that's beyond your grasp.
user: Complexity doesn’t equal quality, my friend. It’s just an excuse for mediocrity wrapped in fancy packaging.
user: Your definition of mediocrity is laughably flawed. I guess subtlety isn't your strong suit.
user: Subtlety or not, the song is a dragging bore that only someone devoid of musical taste could enjoy.
user: So, why don't we settle this by letting everyone hear it and decide for themselves?
user: Fine, but don't come crying when everyone agrees with me. AI, let's get that track playing.

results.prompt:
user: Oh, look who decided to grace us with their presence. Got a license plate issue to brag about, or is this just another one of your 'genius' ideas?
user: Oh, please. Like I'd need your help to resolve a simple license plate mystery. But since you brought it up, what's so difficult about finding a VIN from a plate number?
user: Finding a VIN from a plate number is only tough for someone who can't tell their head from a tailpipe. I bet you don't even know which state you need.
user: Spare me your nonsense. It's obviously California. I suppose you're going to pretend you know how the system works there too?
user: California, huh? Of course, the state where everyone pretends to be a movie star. What's the license plate then, Mr. Perfect? Or is that too much for you to handle?
user: Keep up, will you? It's 8ABC123. Maybe you should focus more on the facts than on your pitiful attempts at insults.
user: Well, at least you managed to remember that much. Shouldn't be too hard for the AI to figure this one out, even with your input.
user: Clearly, even with your constant distractions, the AI won't have trouble sorting this minor issue. Shall we get on with it, or do you need another chance to embarrass yourself?
user: AI, please help us sort out this California license plate issue.

results.prompt:
user: Oh, please, you're not seriously suggesting that a simple bakery can compare to the culinary wonders of Paris, are you? You clearly underestimate the elegance and sophistication required to even mention Parisian bakeries in the same breath.
user: Parisian bakeries probably make your so-called "culinary wonders" look like moldy bread. But sure, let's hear your excuse to avoid finding a decent bakery.
user: The real joke is thinking you can find quality anywhere else but Paris! It's not an excuse; it's common sense to know where real talent lies.
user: Common sense? More like common ignorance! Talent isn't exclusive to Paris, you know. But whatever helps you sleep at night, I guess.
user: Ignorance is thinking you can find a bakery worthy of the name outside Paris. Good luck with your generic options!
user: Generic? Hardly! You've just admitted Paris has the best bakeries. So finding one there won't be an issue for you, right?
user: Finally, some acknowledgment of the truth. Of course, finding one in Paris is no issue—it's simply a matter of knowing where to look.
user: So, you're saying you don't even know how to find one? Typical. Let's just find out which bakery is worth all this bluster.
user: I suppose I could let you in on where to find one, even if it's just to prove a point. Let's get on with it then.
user: Alright, let’s settle this once and for all. AI, help us out in Paris.

results.prompt:
user: Do you know how Instagram's likes system works, especially when it comes to tracking and retrieving those numbers?
user: Instagram uses shortcodes for posts, which are unique identifiers that help track interactions like likes.
user: Can you explain how pagination works in retrieving Instagram data?
user: Pagination involves using a cursor that lets you retrieve data in chunks, so you don't overload with too much information at once.
user: Is there a limit to how many likes you can retrieve from a single Instagram post at one time?
user: Yes, Instagram limits the number of likes you can retrieve per request, typically up to 50.
user: How do you determine which specific likes to retrieve? Is there a starting point specified?
user: The starting point is determined by the end cursor, which marks the last like fetched in the previous request.
user: What role does the shortcode play in this process?
user: The shortcode uniquely identifies each Instagram post, allowing the system to pull likes specifically for that post.
user: If we wanted to retrieve specific likes data, what combination of parameters would we need to provide accurately?
user: We would need to provide the shortcode of the post, the number of likes to retrieve, and an optional end cursor for pagination.
user: For instance, if we have a particular post in mind, can we specify an example set of parameters?
user: Sure, suppose we use the shortcode "B7G5VdKj5nX" and decide to retrieve 30 likes starting from a specific point.
user: And we might use an end cursor like "QVFDd3ZjNk1j" to mark where we last left off.
user: AI, please retrieve the likes data for that particular post.

results.prompt:
user: So, you're trying to find a rental in Central London, are you? Good luck with that! Central London, the place where dreams go to die under the weight of astronomical rents and tiny spaces! Why not look for a "modern apartment"? You know, those tiny boxes they call homes.
user: Tiny boxes? More like modern masterpieces! And besides, what's your issue with looking for an apartment that's actually furnished? Makes moving a lot easier when you don't have to haul furniture around, doesn't it?
user: Furnished or not, the real joke is trying to find something with parking in London! You could be looking until next year. Maybe you should look for a garden instead. Oh wait, good luck with that in the city center!
user: Oh please, a garden in Central London is about as common as a unicorn! You may as well search for a balcony or terrace—at least that's somewhat realistic, if you're willing to pay the price of a small fortune.
user: And let's not forget about bills. Imagine paying that hefty rent and then getting slapped with sky-high utility bills! It's smarter to find a place that includes them, but I guess some people like surprises.
user: Surprises like finding a place that actually allows pets in London? That’s just wishful thinking in most cases. Better stick to dreaming about a max of 3 bedrooms. Anything more and you’d need a miracle.
user: Dreaming, that's exactly what you'd be doing trying to nail down a spot within, say, 30 minutes travel time with most transport types in this city. Tube's your only hope if you don't want to spend your life commuting.
user: A 30-minute commute? Sounds more like a fairytale in the land of endless delays. And don’t get me started on the rent! Anything under 2500 a month might as well be in a different realm altogether.
user: But who am I kidding? You'll never find a "modern apartment" available "immediately" that hasn't been snatched up already. Try looking for places that were listed just "last week" if you're hoping for anything remotely fresh! AI, please start searching for something sensible in that area.
